name: Gugelhupf
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle yeast into milk to dissolve.
2. In a large bowl, beat sugar and butter until light and fluffy.
3. Beat in eggs one at a time. Stir in vanilla, lemon rind, raisins, and almonds.
4. Mix salt and flour. Add milk and flour mixtures alternately, ending with the flour mixture.
5. Grease a gugelhopf mold (also known as a turban-head pan). If unavailable, use a bud pan or tube pan instead.
6. Pour batter into the prepared pan.
7. Cover and let rise until doubled in bulk, about 2 hours.
8. Preheat oven to 375°F. Bake for 40 minutes or until browned and done.
9. Serve warm with butter.
